commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The remaining blocker is a security-boundary design and implementation issue that maintainers should resolve before automation or merge.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a new security boundary, but the scanner can be bypassed by nested shell payloads and unsupported shell expansion.; Review finding: [P1] Inspect carried shell commands before allowing exec — src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts:1307-1314 (sha=bf302c9083b0c65e486f709a116b790c4c76e2b3)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a `tools.exec.deniedPaths` config field, threads it into exec tool creation, documents it, updates generated baseline hashes, and adds a literal-path regression test.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect by source inspection: a command such as `bash -lc 'cat /run/secrets/provider.key'` is scanned as an outer argv payload, so the inner denied path is not matched. I did not execute the PR branch because this review kept the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is a security-boundary design and implementation issue that maintainers should resolve before automation or merge.

target.title: fix(exec): block configured denied path operands
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: issue #11829 calls out agent access to provider/API key material as a security roadmap gap, and the issue discussion proposed a configurable `tools.exec.deniedPaths` surface for tenant-controlled secret paths.
- Solution: add `tools.exec.deniedPaths` as an exec preflight guard that rejects commands referencing configured paths such as `/run/secrets/**` before gateway/node/sandbox execution.
- What changed: runtime guard, global/agent config wiring, zod/type/schema help/labels/tags, docs, generated config baseline, and a regression test.
- What did NOT change: no changes to model catalog filtering, auth proxy docs, or the existing models.json key-stripping work already covered by other PRs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #11829
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

- This gives operators a config-level guard for mounted secret directories, especially deployments that keep provider credentials in predictable paths such as `/run/secrets/**`. It is defense-in-depth rather than a replacement for sandboxing or secret isolation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: exec commands can currently reference mounted sensitive paths unless deployment isolation catches the access; after this patch a configured denied path is rejected before host execution.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux with Node 22.22.0, invoking the real `createExecTool` runtime with `host: "gateway"`, `security: "full"`, `ask: "off"`, and `deniedPaths: ["/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/**"]`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx` importing `./src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts`, creating the exec tool, and calling `tool.execute("real-behavior-proof", { command: "cat \"/tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key\"" })`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied console output from the local runtime invocation:

```text
blocked: Security Violation: exec command references denied path /tmp/openclaw-denied-proof/provider.key
```

- Observed result after fix: the exec tool throws before running the `cat` command, so the denied path is not handed to gateway execution.
- What was not tested: a full multi-tenant Docker deployment with real mounted provider secrets.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the regression test failed before implementation because the same denied-path command resolved successfully with `(no output)` instead of rejecting.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: exec had approval/safe-bin/env guards, but no config-level path denylist for sensitive operand paths.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression test asserted that a configured secret path is blocked before host execution.
- Contributing context (if known): issue #11829 covers a broader roadmap; this PR implements only the `tools.exec.deniedPaths` part.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a gateway/full/off exec command targeting a configured denied `/**` path rejects with `Security Violation` before host execution.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises `createExecTool` directly and proves the preflight guard runs even when approvals are bypassed.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New optional config key: `tools.exec.deniedPaths`. Commands that reference matching paths now fail before exec starts.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
exec command -> env/path setup -> host execution may receive /run/secrets/provider.key

After:
exec command -> env/path setup -> denied path preflight -> Security Violation before host execution
```

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts`
- `pnpm config:schema:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `pnpm lint:core`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-types.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.ts src/config/types.tools.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/schema.tags.ts docs/tools/exec.md docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`
- `git diff --check`
